title: Lab Setup Guide
when_to_use: Before starting any hands-on offensive security practice

steps.title: Install Hypervisor
steps.instruction: Download and install VirtualBox (virtualbox.org) or VMware
steps.tip: VirtualBox is free and works on all platforms. VMware is faster but requires a license for full features

steps.title: Download Kali
steps.instruction: Download Kali Linux ISO from kali.org/get-kali
steps.tip: Use the installer ISO, not the live ISO, for a persistent installation with better performance

steps.title: Create Kali VM
steps.instruction: Create new VM: 2 CPUs, 4GB RAM minimum, 40GB disk
steps.tip: 4GB RAM is minimum. 8GB is recommended for running multiple tools simultaneously

steps.title: Install & Update
steps.instruction: Install Kali, update: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade
steps.tip: Run updates after every fresh install. Many tools receive critical patches regularly

steps.title: Deploy Target
steps.instruction: Download Metasploitable 2 or DVWA Docker image
steps.tip: Metasploitable has multiple services. DVWA focuses on web. Ideally deploy both for variety

steps.title: Configure Network
steps.instruction: Set both VMs to Host-Only network adapter
steps.tip: Host-Only isolates VMs from your real network. Critical for security when running vulnerable targets

steps.title: Test & Snapshot
steps.instruction: Test connectivity: ping from Kali to target, then take clean snapshots
steps.tip: Snapshots are your safety net. Take one after initial setup and after each major configuration change
